alkylation reagent: the addition of 100 mM iodoacetamide in 100 mM ammonium bicarbonate for 30 min to form carbamidomethyl cysteine.
alkylation concentration: the addition of 100 mM iodoacetamide in 100 mM ammonium bicarbonate for 30 min to form carbamidomethyl cysteine.
cleavage agent: 300 ng of trypsin in 50 mM ammonium bicarbonate to the dried gel pieces.
collision energy: collision energy (HCD), 28% or 30% (for UV- and mechlorethamine-cross-linked samples, respectively);
enrichment method: enrichment of cross-linked peptide-(oligo)nucleotides was performed as described using Titansphere TiO2 10 nm beads.
fragmentation method: collision energy (HCD), 28% or 30% (for UV- and mechlorethamine-cross-linked samples, respectively);
instrument: mass spectrometry was performed using an Orbitrap Eclipse mass spectrometer...
labeling: total RNA was collected for spike-in normalized RNA sequencing.
reduction reagent: 30 μl of 10 mM dithiothreitol (DTT) in 100 mM ammonium bicarbonate for 30 min to reduce disulfide bonds.
reduction concentration: 30 μl of 10 mM dithiothreitol (DTT) in 100 mM ammonium bicarbonate for 30 min to reduce disulfide bonds.
